content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Removed the unsound idempotent-retry fallback from claim_bead_in_br. The previous fallback checked is_our_active_bead (controller in Claimed state with matching bead_id) to accept an already-in_progress bead as our own prior claim. This was unsound because select_next_milestone_bead writes the Claimed controller state during selection, before any br update — so a matching controller merely proves local selection, not a prior successful br claim. Another operator could win the race after our selection but before our update. Now claim_bead_in_br always fails if br update fails, regardless of bead status or controller state. The is_our_active_bead helper, install_fake_br_claim_idempotent fixture, and two dependent tests were removed. A new race condition test (claim_fails_even_when_controller_already_claimed_the_bead) explicitly documents that local selection does not count as proof of a prior br claim.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Remove is_our_active_bead() helper function that checked controller Claimed state for ownership — unsound because Claimed state is set during selection, not after br update
2. [Completed] Simplify claim_bead_in_br to always fail on br update error — remove load_bead_detail fallback, match arms, and idempotent-retry logic. Remove milestone_id parameter since ownership check is gone
3. [Completed] Update claim_bead_in_br call site in handle_create_from_bead to remove milestone_id parameter
4. [Completed] Remove install_fake_br_claim_idempotent fixture (no longer needed)
5. [Completed] Simplify install_fake_br_claim_failure fixture — no longer needs bead_id parameter or show handler since no fallback check occurs
6. [Completed] Remove test: claim_bead_in_br_succeeds_idempotently_when_our_own_prior_claim (behavior removed)
7. [Completed] Remove test: claim_bead_in_br_rejects_bead_claimed_by_another_process (behavior merged into general failure)
8. [Completed] Add test: claim_fails_even_when_controller_already_claimed_the_bead — documents the race condition: controller is Claimed with matching bead, but br update still fails because another operator won the race. No idempotent retry allowed
9. [Completed] Remove milestone_id and ensure_milestone_dir from tests that no longer need controller setup (claim success/failure tests)
10. [Completed] Run cargo test — 1582 total tests pass (658 lib + 166 cli + 1 tmux + 757 unit), 0 failures
11. [Completed] Verify cargo fmt --check: clean
12. [Completed] Verify cargo clippy: 19 pre-existing errors, no new violations

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 1582 total tests pass (658 lib + 166 cli + 1 tmux + 757 unit), 0 failures
- cargo fmt --check: clean (no formatting issues)
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings: 19 actual errors (+ 1 'could not compile' summary), all pre-existing
- claim_bead_in_br_succeeds_on_update_and_sync: passes (no milestone_id param)
- claim_bead_in_br_fails_when_update_exits_nonzero: passes (simplified fixture)
- claim_fails_even_when_controller_already_claimed_the_bead: NEW — passes, explicitly proves race condition is handled correctly — local Claimed selection does not bypass br update failure
- claim_failure_transitions_controller_to_needs_operator: passes
- successful_claim_records_task_id_in_controller: passes (updated claim call)

## Outstanding Risks

- All 19 clippy errors remain pre-existing (identical set to base commit); no new violations introduced
